description: The Montenegrin mafia refers to criminal organizations based in Montenegro or composed of Montenegrins, active since 1992. They specialize in narcotics smuggling, tobacco smuggling, and arms trafficking, with the Kavač and Škaljari clans being the most prominent. These groups are deeply involved in the Balkan drug trade and have alliances with Italian mafias and Latin American cartels. Corruption and weak state institutions have allowed them to thrive.

country_links.evidence_quote: both clans maintain a direct presence in South America - port cities in Brazil , border regions of Paraguay , Uruguay and Argentina ; with some presence registered in Ecuador and Peru .
country_links.context: Both clans maintain a direct presence in border regions of Argentina.

country_links.evidence_quote: Both clans have offshoots and associates in Balkans region; European strongholds include ... Austria , Netherlands , Germany , Belgium , Greece , Hungary , Slovakia , Ukraine and Turkey .
country_links.context: Both clans have offshoots and associates in Austria.
